Source_Species.reason: LS was studied in Schizonepeta tenuifolia throughout the paper.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The paper explicitly names the species as Schizonepeta tenuifolia.

Applied_Species.reason: Experiments on LS were conducted in S. tenuifolia plants.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The applied species in the experiments is explicitly Schizonepeta tenuifolia.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: LS is directly linked to limonene and discussed as the first committed step toward menthone, but LS does not directly form menthone.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Limonene and menthone are described as monoterpenoid/terpenoid compounds.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The summary states volatile oils including limonene, pulegone and menthone are major constituents determining medicinal value and consumer popularity.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Metabolite profiling was performed on leaves, and LS-related analyses used leaf samples.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: LS is placed in the menthone biosynthesis pathway/monoterpenoid biosynthesis pathway in Results and figures.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: The Discussion explicitly states LS is the first committed step in the MBP, i.e. branch entry toward limonene/menthone biosynthesis.

Enzyme_Name.reason: LS is explicitly expanded as limonene synthase.

EC_Number.reason: No EC number for LS appears in the paper text.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: LS is an enzyme in monoterpene biosynthesis, but no explicit protein family/domain annotation such as terpene synthase family is provided.

Primary_Substrate.reason: The Discussion states LS converts GPP into limonene.

Primary_Product.reason: The paper explicitly states LS is the key enzyme in limonene biosynthesis and converts GPP into limonene.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: The Discussion states LS converts GPP into limonene and is the first committed step in the MBP.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: The paper explicitly describes LS as the first committed step in the MBP, i.e. upstream/committed.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: LS directly forms limonene and is described as the first committed step leading toward menthone biosynthesis.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: LS is explicitly called the key enzyme in limonene biosynthesis and the first committed step in the MBP.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results show LS transcript/protein are higher in light/HL, lower in dark, and dark increases m6A enrichment on LS mRNA consistent with reduced accumulation.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: LS was examined by transcriptome, RT-qPCR, proteomics, m6A-IP-qPCR, VIGS of writer genes, LS degradation assay in Figure S6b, and GC-MS metabolite profiling.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: MTA, HAKAI and VIR regulate LS-related outcomes, but direct physical interaction of LS with these proteins was not shown.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: Results state LS mRNA showed light-specific upregulation; proteomics also showed higher LS abundance in HL and reduced abundance in dark.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Leaf analyses under GL/HL/dark showed LS higher in light and reduced in dark.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: These exact light treatments were the core experimental factors.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The paper proposes engineering LS, PR and m6A synergistically, but the specific phrasing about higher limonene/monoterpenoid output and redirecting flux is interpretive.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Results show silencing writer genes raised LS in dark but lowered LS in GL, demonstrating context-dependent effects.

Omics_Data.reason: The paper gives transcriptome accession GSA: CRA020843 and proteomics dataset PXD051576.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Results and Discussion state LS mRNA is highly enriched for m6A in dark, suggest negative regulation via stability in dark, and note the opposite effect in light upon writer silencing.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three summary points are directly supported: LS as first committed enzyme converting GPP to limonene, light-correlated transcript/protein with limonene, and dark-associated m6A/post-transcriptional regulation.

corrections.reason: No EC number for LS appears in the paper text.

Source_Species.reason: PR was studied in Schizonepeta tenuifolia.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The paper explicitly names Schizonepeta tenuifolia.

Applied_Species.reason: All PR experiments were done in S. tenuifolia, with one protein assay in Arabidopsis protoplasts for heterologous expression.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The main applied species for PR regulation is Schizonepeta tenuifolia.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: PR is directly linked to menthone and catalyzes pulegone to menthone conversion.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Menthone is described as a terpenoid/monoterpenoid.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Menthone is included among major volatile oils determining medicinal value and consumer popularity.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Menthone measurements were made from leaves, and PR analyses used leaves.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: PR is discussed as part of the MBP/monoterpenoid biosynthesis pathway.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: PR is explicitly the terminal menthone-forming step in the pathway.

Enzyme_Name.reason: PR is explicitly expanded as pulegone reductase.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: PR is a reductase and catalyzes a reduction, but no explicit family/domain annotation is provided in the paper.

Primary_Substrate.reason: The paper explicitly states PR catalyzes reduction of pulegone.

Primary_Product.reason: The paper explicitly states PR forms menthone from pulegone.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: The Discussion says PR catalyzes stereoselective reduction of pulegone to menthone.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: PR is described as the terminal enzymatic step of menthone biosynthesis.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: PR directly forms the target product menthone.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: Results/Discussion describe PR as a key enzyme and terminal enzyme strongly associated with menthone accumulation.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results show dark increases PR transcript/protein and menthone, and reduced m6A via writer silencing increases PR stability and protein accumulation.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods are reported for PR, including transcriptome, RT-qPCR, proteomics, m6A-IP-qPCR, VIGS, mRNA degradation, cleavage assays, motif mutagenesis/protoplast assay, and GC-MS.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: MTA, HAKAI and VIR affect PR regulation, but direct PR-protein interaction with these factors was not shown.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: PR transcript and protein are reported higher in dark and lower in GL/HL.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Leaf-based assays showed highest PR abundance under dark treatment.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: These exact light regimes were used for PR analyses.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The paper explicitly proposes engineering menthone production through PR biogenesis and editing/controlling m6A-related mechanisms.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Results show PR transcript levels changed mainly in dark after writer silencing and not significantly in GL, indicating condition dependence.

Omics_Data.reason: The transcriptome and proteome accession identifiers are explicitly given.

Other_Important_Info.reason: These mechanistic PR findings are all directly reported in Results/Figure 7.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: Each summary point matches Results and Figure 7: PR terminal reaction, dark-enhanced transcript/protein/menthone, and negative regulation by m6A-mediated degradation/cleavage.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The species is explicitly Schizonepeta tenuifolia.

Regulator_Type.reason: MTA is explicitly described as an m6A writer gene.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: MTA is homologous to Arabidopsis m6A machinery, but the paper does not explicitly call it METTL3-like.

Regulation_Mode.reason: The study links m6A writer genes including MTA to post-transcriptional regulation affecting transcript stability/degradation.

Metabolic_Process_Controlled.reason: Silencing MTA alters monoterpenoid accumulation and transcript levels of MBP genes.

Primary_Regulatory_Targets.reason: MTA affects PR and LS-related expression patterns, but direct target specificity for MTA alone was not mechanistically demonstrated.

Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes.reason: Results show MTA silencing increases PR transcript in dark and has context-dependent effects on LS, including downregulation in GL.

Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs.reason: MTA expression is described as positively associated with light intensity.

Protein_Complex_Involvement.reason: The paper groups MTA, HAKAI and VIR as m6A writers/writer genes, but does not directly demonstrate a physical complex in S. tenuifolia.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: No direct interaction assays between MTA and HAKAI/VIR are shown.

Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product.reason: Silencing MTA significantly increased menthone, limonene and pulegone in VIGS plants.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: These metabolites were directly measured and increased after MTA silencing.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The paper states these volatile oils contribute to medicinal value and popularity/quality.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results explicitly state silencing MTA enhanced monoterpenoid accumulation under dark and GL.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: MTA was tested by RT-qPCR expression profiling, VIGS, GC-MS, and RT-qPCR of pathway genes.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: Figure 5c text says MTA expression was positively associated with light intensity.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: This directly implies higher MTA under light than dark.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Darkness and growth light were explicit VIGS treatment conditions.

Epigenetic_Regulation.reason: MTA is part of the m6A RNA modification machinery, i.e. epitranscriptomic regulation.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Broad pleiotropic effects are plausible, but the paper does not directly characterize them for MTA beyond multiple metabolite/transcript changes.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The study proposes manipulating m6A machinery to enhance terpenoid/monoterpenoid production.

Omics_Data.reason: The accessions CRA020843 and PXD051576 are explicitly provided.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The paper indeed notes VIR is more mechanistically resolved than the other writers, while MTA still shows metabolite/transcript effects.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three statements are supported by Figure 5c, Figure 6, and accompanying text.

Source_Species.reason: VIR was studied in Schizonepeta tenuifolia.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The species is explicitly Schizonepeta tenuifolia.

Regulator_Type.reason: VIR is discussed as an m6A writer gene/writer-related factor.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: The paper names VIR but does not explicitly annotate it as VIRILIZER-like.

Regulation_Mode.reason: Results show VIR affects m6A deposition, cleavage, stability and protein accumulation of PR transcripts.

Primary_Regulatory_Targets.reason: PR is mechanistically validated, and LS is also discussed as affected by writer dysfunction.

Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes.reason: The paper shows VIR silencing lowers m6A on PR and increases PR stability/abundance in dark; LS degradation is stated to show a similar mechanism.

Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs.reason: VIR expression is significantly higher in Dark than in GL and HL.

Protein_Complex_Involvement.reason: The paper suggests common writer action with MTA and HAKAI, but does not directly demonstrate a physical complex in S. tenuifolia.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: No direct protein-protein interaction assays between VIR and MTA/HAKAI are shown.

Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product.reason: VIR is the most mechanistically validated writer affecting menthone through PR regulation.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: Menthone, limonene and pulegone all increased upon VIR silencing.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: These are the monoterpenoid/terpenoid compounds studied.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: They are described as major active volatile oils related to medicinal value/quality/popularity.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Leaf tissue was used for VIR VIGS metabolite measurements.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods are reported for VIR-related validation.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: Figure 5c text says VIR is significantly higher in Dark than GL and HL.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: The reported expression pattern is in leaf samples under dark.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Darkness and growth light are explicit VIR test conditions; light intensity is central to the study.

Epigenetic_Regulation.reason: VIR is part of the m6A epitranscriptomic regulation examined here.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Broad effects beyond PR/LS are plausible, but this broad-target tradeoff is not directly characterized in the paper.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The paper proposes manipulating m6A machinery and even specific m6A motifs to enhance menthone production.

Omics_Data.reason: The paper explicitly provides CRA020843 and PXD051576.

Other_Important_Info.reason: These PR-focused VIR mechanistic findings are directly shown in Figure 7 and text.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three statements match the Results and model: VIR controls monoterpenoid biosynthesis, silencing raises the metabolites, and VIR promotes PR m6A-linked cleavage/degradation in dark.
